name: Cheese (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight; representative aged/hard cheese (cheddar-type) used as the reference matrix for generic 'Cheese'

components.name: water
components.evidence: moisture of a typical cheddar-type cheese

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2023, Food Sci Nutr, PMC10171539 (HPLC, cheddar)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid and principal fermentable carbon/energy source in cheese; product of starter-culture lactose fermentation. Range across cheeses 0.27 (Swiss) to 1.89 (Parmesan) %. Was MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: second most abundant organic acid; from heterofermentative/citrate metabolism. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: propionic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2023, PMC10171539 (HPLC, cheddar; up to 0.63% in Swiss)
components.evidence: propionibacteria fermentation product; defining acid of Swiss-type cheese. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: butyric acid (free)
components.evidence: free C4 from lipolysis / fermentation (distinct from esterified C4:0 in milk fat); much higher in Parmesan (2.86%) and Swiss (0.89%). Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: residual milk citrate; substrate for diacetyl/CO2 production. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.evidence: fermentation intermediate. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: formic acid
components.source: Bevilacqua & Califano 1989 / Marsili HPLC cheddar (0.28-0.50 mg/g)
components.evidence: minor fermentation acid; midpoint of 28-50 mg/100g. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: orotic acid
components.evidence: characteristic dairy pyrimidine acid. Missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: lactose
components.evidence: residual only; most lactose is fermented to lactic acid during ripening (0 in Swiss/Mozzarella). Fresh/unripened cheeses retain more.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: free galactose from lactose hydrolysis by galactose-negative starters; the most abundant residual monosaccharide in cheese.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: not detected in ripened cheese (fully metabolized).

components.name: total protein (casein-derived)
components.evidence: predominantly casein; extensively hydrolyzed to peptides + free amino acids during ripening (aged cheese has high free-amino-acid pools). Range ~23-25 g/100g.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: most abundant amino acid; large free-glutamate pool in aged cheese (umami).

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: casein-rich; high free proline in aged cheese.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: milk fat; source of free fatty acids on lipolysis

components.name: hexadecanoic acid (C16:0, palmitic)
components.evidence: MAJOR fatty acid, largest single FA; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: octadecanoic acid (C18:0, stearic)
components.evidence: MAJOR fatty acid; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: tetradecanoic acid (C14:0, myristic)
components.evidence: MAJOR fatty acid; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: butanoic acid (C4:0, butyric, esterified)
components.evidence: characteristic short-chain milk-fat FA; was MISSING from FooDB profile

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: GENUINE animal sterol of a dairy food (kept; not a plant-food artifact)

components.name: phosphate (phosphorus)
components.evidence: as phosphorus; colloidal calcium phosphate + organic P

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: added salt (NaCl); high and variable by variety

components.name: chloride
components.source: estimated from added NaCl (~654 mg Na)
components.evidence: counter-ion of salt; approximate stoichiometric estimate, variety-dependent

components.name: iodine
components.source: UK McCance & Widdowson / EFSA dairy iodine data
components.evidence: genuine and significant in dairy; typical hard-cheese value ~30 ug/100g (variable)

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: GENUINE milk-fat carotenoid giving cheese its yellow color; kept (alpha-carotene/cryptoxanthin only trace)

components.name: biotin (vitamin B7)
components.source: UK CoFID / FRIDA dairy biotin data
components.evidence: genuine in cheese ~2-4 ug/100g; USDA leaves biotin unquantified

components.name: vitamin K2 (menaquinone-4, MK-4)
components.evidence: dominant K vitamer in cheese; fermentation/animal origin

components.name: vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol)
components.evidence: small amount from milk fat (~24 IU)

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: dairy osmolyte / compatible solute; retained but not quantified

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: cheese contains no starch; implausible for a dairy matrix (FooDB artifact)

remove.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not present in cheese; milk sugar is lactose only, and it is fermented away (FooDB artifact)

remove.name: Maltose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not present in cheese; no starch hydrolysis source (FooDB artifact)

remove.name: L-Ascorbate
remove.reason: vitamin C is ~0 mg/100g in cheese; not a real nutrient of this food (FooDB artifact)

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: EPA (C20:5 n-3) is a marine fatty acid present only at negligible trace in ruminant milk fat; not a defining or growth-relevant nutrient of cheese

remove.name: Doconexent
remove.reason: DHA (C22:6 n-3) is a marine fatty acid, negligible trace in milk fat; not growth-relevant (FooDB over-listing)

remove.name: Alpha-Carotene
remove.reason: only trace in milk fat; effectively negligible vs beta-carotene which is kept (predicted/over-listed)

remove.name: Beta-Cryptoxanthin
remove.reason: negligible trace carotenoid in cheese; not growth-relevant (over-listed)

remove.name: Gadoleic acid
remove.reason: C20:1 present only at trace in milk fat; not growth-relevant

remove.name: Gamolenic acid
remove.reason: GLA (C18:3 n-6) negligible trace in milk fat; not a defining nutrient

missing_added: lactic acid (dominant organic acid / main fermentable carbon source)
D-galactose and residual lactose with real amounts (replacing sucrose/maltose/starch)
palmitic acid C16:0 (largest single fatty acid)
vitamin K2 (MK-4) split from total K
keep_uncovered: epsilon-Polylysine (genuine antimicrobial peptide additive in some cheeses; no simple BiGG met)
Menatetrenone (vitamin K2 MK-4 — quantified above as a component; stays uncovered in BiGG)
beta-Lactose (stereoform of the lactose already listed)
24,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3 (genuine minor vitamin-D metabolite; no BiGG met)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source for a microbe growing on cheese is LACTIC ACID (~1.4 g/100g), not sugar: lactose is almost fully fermented during ripening, leaving only residual lactose (~0.1 g) and free galactose (~0.27 g). After lactate, the growth substrates are the free-amino-acid/peptide pool from casein proteolysis (glutamate, proline, leucine highest) and milk-fat free fatty acids from lipolysis. Composition modeled on aged cheddar-type cheese as the generic 'Cheese' reference; organic-acid and residual-sugar figures vary strongly by variety (e.g. Swiss is propionate-rich and lactate-poor, Parmesan is butyrate/lactate-rich).
sources: USDA FDC 328637 (Cheese, cheddar) — macronutrients, fatty acids, minerals, most vitamins
USDA FDC 173414 (SR Legacy, Cheese, cheddar) — full amino-acid profile, choline, vitamin D, beta-carotene, C15:0
USDA FDC 171292 (Cheese, cheddar reduced fat) — vitamin D / choline cross-check
Zhang et al. 2023, Food Science & Nutrition, PMC10171539 — HPLC carbohydrates + organic acids across cheddar/parmesan/mozzarella/swiss
Marsili et al. HPLC organic acids in cheese/milk/whey (J Food Sci) — lactic/citric/acetic/formic cross-check
FooDB FOOD00617 (Cheese) — component inventory being curated
UK McCance & Widdowson CoFID / EFSA + FRIDA — dairy iodine and biotin
